content: We can reframe second-language vocabulary acquisition (the educational reflexion context) as an iterative LLM-style self-refinement process, where a learner’s evolving understanding of a word like 'reflection' itself is refined through repeated, self-guided reflexion cycles. A learner does not master a word after one definition, but instead improves their grasp of its nuance across contexts via self-feedback that mirrors how Self-Refine improves LLM outputs.
